title: Source Reference Hardening Checklist
principle: A source-reference record must be hardened before it can be used as source evidence for a Word record. AG69H defines the hardening checklist but does not promote source records.

checklist.requirement: Source URL or repository reference must be reachable or independently locatable.
Publisher/institution must be recorded and credible for the relevant source category.
Source scope must match the evidence claim: Sanskrit form, Hindi form, meaning, corpus attestation, Vedic context, or published textual discipline.
Reuse note must confirm metadata-only use or permitted excerpt limits; bulk ingestion remains blocked.
Public claim remains blocked unless source is approved and record-level evidence supports the exact claim.
Internal textual discipline or Nityanand Misra aligned published-work handling cannot become public attribution without exact work verification and public-use approval.
